summary: Phase-2 F0 STABILITY fix (whole-take mean F0 + FIXED speaker-group thresholds men<165<=women<=255<children) verified end-to-end via /api/phonemes/analyze-formants. Extended /app/backend/tests/test_phoneme_formants_phase2.py from 23 -> 29 tests. Added 4 new test classes covering all the review-request requirements: (1) F0 STABILITY — same /ʊ/-like F0=120 WAV posted 3x in one session yields IDENTICAL F0 and reference_group='men' across all runs; (2) F0 THRESHOLDS (parametrized) — F0=120→men, F0=200→women, F0=280→children, with F0 measured within ±10 Hz of synthesised value and cross-checked against the fixed thresholds; (3) WHOLE-TAKE MEAN F0 log line — backend log contains 'F0=<val> selected_group=<grp> (via f0_threshold)' whenever F0 is present; (4) MIN-100ms voiced fallback — short (<100 ms voiced) or noise-only clip yields F0=None and group_method='formant_distance' in the log while HTTP 200 with a valid reference_group is still returned. Also fixed one pre-existing assertion in TestBug4VowelNucleusIgnoresLouderUnvoicedBurst that still searched for the old log substring '(via f0)'; the router now emits '(via f0_threshold)'. 29/29 passing (was 22/23 before the fix + new tests).
critical_code_review_comments: phoneme_formants.py:_extract_formants — F0 is now the arithmetic mean of ALL voiced grid samples. This is correct for stability, but the mean is sensitive to octave errors in Praat's pitch tracker on very low SNR clips. Consider switching to the *median* over voiced frames as a defensive follow-up (it's what the nucleus formants already use); it would keep the same ±1 Hz reproducibility while being robust to a handful of stray octave-jumped frames.

The FIXED thresholds (<165 men, 165–255 women, >255 children) are hard-coded inline in analyze_formants. They match the review spec, but consider promoting them to module-level constants (e.g. F0_MAN_MAX=165, F0_WOMAN_MAX=255) so a future linguist-tunable config or dialect-specific override can be introduced without touching the endpoint logic.

The 100 ms minimum voiced-duration gate is derived from 'len(voiced_f0) * step' where step=0.005 s. Making the constant explicit (MIN_VOICED_SEC=0.10) and logging when it triggers (e.g. 'F0 skipped: only 60 ms voiced') would help future field-debugging of noisy student mics.

For RP the mapping women→female and children→female both collapse onto the same reference row, so a female student with F0>255 is compared to the 'female' reference (correct fallback, but subtly loses child-vs-adult contrast). This is acceptable given RP data availability, but worth a debug-log note when children→female mapping is used.

TestPhase2Min100msVoicedFallback needed a two-stage attempt (short voiced burst then noise-only) because Praat's pitch tracker can occasionally still find >100 ms of voiced frames in a padded 60 ms burst. This is a test-robustness concern, not a code bug — the F0=None branch itself is exercised deterministically for pure noise.
seed_data_creation: None — reused existing formant_references seeds (AmE men/women/children; RP male/female) and the pre-seeded teacher u-foot mp3 sample.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_phoneme_formants_phase2.py now has 29 tests (was 23). New Phase-2 F0-stability classes: TestPhase2F0Stability3IdenticalPosts (1 test — 3× identical POST → identical F0 and group), TestPhase2F0FixedThresholds (3 parametrised tests — F0=120→men, 200→women, 280→children), TestPhase2WholeTakeMeanF0Logging (1 test — log line has F0=<v> and (via f0_threshold)), TestPhase2Min100msVoicedFallback (1 test — <100 ms voiced → F0=None, group_method='formant_distance', still HTTP 200). New helpers: _synthesize_voiced_only_wav (short burst padded with silence) and _synthesize_noise_only_wav (unvoiced white noise, guarantees zero voiced frames). Also fixed one pre-existing assertion in TestBug4VowelNucleusIgnoresLouderUnvoicedBurst: log marker updated from '(via f0)' to '(via f0_threshold)' to match the new router log format. To run: cd /app/backend && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://canonical-voice-lab.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest tests/test_phoneme_formants_phase2.py -v
rca of the issue: Root cause (as fixed by the main agent in /app/backend/routers/phoneme_formants.py): F0 was previously estimated as the median over the ~20 ms vowel nucleus only, which is highly sensitive to microphone noise, breathiness at vowel edges, and Praat pitch-tracker jitter. Two consecutive recordings of the same speaker could report F0=118 in one take and F0=170 in the next, flipping the speaker-group classification (men vs women) and therefore the reference means used for the GOP score — a silent, unreproducible failure mode. The fix (verified in this iteration): (1) chosen['F0'] is now the arithmetic MEAN over ALL voiced grid samples of the entire recording (see lines 188–192 of phoneme_formants.py), requiring >=100 ms of voiced audio (0.100 s = 20 grid frames at 5 ms step) else F0 is set to None. (2) Group selection uses FIXED thresholds on that mean F0: f0<165 → men, 165<=f0<=255 → women, f0>255 → children; RP dataset (male/female only) maps women/children → female. (3) group_method is 'f0_threshold' when F0 is present, 'formant_distance' when F0 is None (fallback to nearest-formant-mean group). The log line 'analyze-formants: phoneme=… F0=… selected_group=… (via …)' now uniquely identifies which path was taken. Reproduction: 3 identical POSTs of the same F0=120 /ʊ/-like WAV now yield IDENTICAL F0 (120 ± 0) and IDENTICAL group ('men') across all runs. F0=200 → 'women', F0=280 → 'children'. A 60 ms voiced burst (< 100 ms gate) or pure noise → F0=None → group_method='formant_distance', HTTP 200 with a valid reference_group still returned. Regression checks unchanged and all passing: F1>900 sanity discard, /iː/ RP low F1, consent gate 403→200, diphthong /aɪ/ absent-from-dataset fallback to teacher_sample, _weighted_composite([0,59,88]) = 62.5 ≥ 60.
